name: E03S06 — En Route Customer SMS

nodes.name: Poll: Every 30 Seconds
nodes.notes:
AC1: Triggers the polling loop every 30 seconds.
Looks for jobs with status='En Route' and en_route_sms_sent=false.
The en_route_sms_sent flag is the idempotency gate — not the trigger interval.

ACTIVATION: Do NOT activate this workflow until:
  1. supabase migration E03S06-add-en-route-sms-sent.sql has been run
  2. SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER replaced in all HTTP Request nodes
  3. FROM_NUMBER_PLACEHOLDER in 'Send Customer SMS' replaced with approved Twilio 10DLC number
  4. Twilio credentials configured in n8n (Settings → Credentials → Twilio)

nodes.name: GET Jobs Pending En Route SMS
nodes.notes:
AC1: Queries jobs table for rows where status='En Route' AND en_route_sms_sent=false.
Returns only required fields: id, client_id, customer_name, customer_phone.
Returns an array (possibly empty) — empty array handled by Split In Items.

URL encoding: 'En Route' is encoded as 'En%20Route' in the URL.

SUPABASE HTTP REQUEST PATTERN (DEC-003): HTTP Request node — NOT native Supabase node.
IMPORTANT: Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER with your anon key from:
  Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API → anon public

RLS REQUIREMENT: anon SELECT policy must exist on jobs table.
  Verify: Supabase → Auth → Policies → jobs → SELECT (anon) exists.
  This policy was confirmed in E02S05 delivery — should already be active.

Reference: $('GET Jobs Pending En Route SMS').item.json.id

nodes.name: Split In Items
nodes.notes:
Processes each matching job independently — one at a time.
If GET Jobs returns empty array, this node outputs nothing and the workflow
completes silently (no SMS, no PATCH, no error).

Batch size 1: each iteration handles exactly one job, keeping error isolation clean.
A failure on one job (e.g. client_id lookup fails) does not block processing of others.

nodes.name: GET Client Details
nodes.notes:
AC3: Fetches business_name and twilio_number from clients table by client_id.
business_name is NEVER hardcoded — always pulled from Supabase (AC3).
twilio_number: the client's 10DLC number (stored in clients.twilio_number).

IMPORTANT: Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER with your anon key.
RLS SELECT on clients table must allow anon reads (established in Sprint 1).

Reference: $('GET Client Details').item.json.business_name
Reference: $('GET Client Details').item.json.twilio_number

nodes.name: Customer Phone Present?
nodes.notes:
AC5: Checks whether jobs.customer_phone is non-null and non-empty.
True branch (phone present): proceed to Build SMS Body → Send Customer SMS → PATCH
False branch (phone null/empty): skip SMS entirely → go directly to PATCH to mark sent

AC5 requirement: 'If customer_phone is null, skip SMS — workflow must not error.'
Skipping to PATCH ensures the job is marked and won't be re-processed on next poll.

nodes.name: Build SMS Body
nodes.notes:
AC2: Assembles exact canonical template: '[Business Name]: Your technician is on the way.'
AC3: business_name read from $('GET Client Details').item.json.business_name — never hardcoded.

Fields returned:
  smsBody: the complete SMS text
  customerPhone: jobs.customer_phone (the destination number — E.164 format expected)
  twilioFromNumber: clients.twilio_number (the From number for Twilio)

IMPORTANT — n8n 2.x reference pattern:
  ALWAYS: $('Node Name').item.json.field
  NEVER:  $json.field for upstream node data
  Code node output: return [{ json: { ... } }]
  Expression mode: toggle orange '=' icon per field

nodes.name: Send Customer SMS
nodes.notes:
AC4: Native n8n Twilio node — NOT HTTP Request (conventions.md, DEC-006).
AC6: continueOnFail=true — Twilio send failure does NOT revert job status.

FROM number: FROM_NUMBER_PLACEHOLDER
  *** REPLACE THIS before activating ***
  Twilio A2P 10DLC campaign not yet approved — using placeholder.
  Replace with the approved 10DLC number in E.164 format: e.g. +13212489804
  The client's twilio_number from the clients table is the intended source
  but is stored as reference only — the From number used in actual Twilio
  sends must be an approved campaign-registered number.

TO number: jobs.customer_phone (from Build SMS Body output)
MESSAGE: canonical En Route template assembled in Build SMS Body

CREDENTIAL: Twilio (Account SID + Auth Token)
  Configure: n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → Twilio
  Update credential id 'twilio-credentials' to match your n8n credential ID.

nodes.name: PATCH jobs — Mark En Route SMS Sent
nodes.notes:
AC1: Writes ONLY en_route_sms_sent=true — job status field is NEVER modified here.
  jobs.status remains 'En Route' regardless of SMS outcome.
AC6: This PATCH writes the sentinel, not the status. SMS delivery outcome has no
  effect on the job status field.

continueOnFail=true: if PATCH fails, workflow does not error. Worst case: duplicate
  SMS on next poll cycle. The canonical template (AC2) is idempotent for the customer.

Both paths merge here:
  - SMS sent path (from Send Customer SMS)
  - Phone null/skip path (from Customer Phone Present? false branch)

SUPABASE PATTERN (DEC-003): HTTP Request — NOT native Supabase node.
IMPORTANT: Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER.
RLS REQUIREMENT: anon UPDATE policy on jobs table must exist.
  Confirmed by E03S05 delivery (tech dashboard PATCH works with anon key).

main.node: GET Jobs Pending En Route SMS
PATCH jobs — Mark En Route SMS Sent
PATCH jobs — Mark En Route SMS Sent

_comment:
INTEGRATION NOTES — READ BEFORE IMPORTING

== PRE-ACTIVATION CHECKLIST ==
  1. Run Supabase migration:
       supabase/migrations/E03S06-add-en-route-sms-sent.sql
     (Adds en_route_sms_sent boolean DEFAULT false to jobs table)
     Run via: Supabase Dashboard → SQL Editor → paste and execute

  2. Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER in ALL THREE HTTP Request nodes:
       - 'GET Jobs Pending En Route SMS'
       - 'GET Client Details'
       - 'PATCH jobs — Mark En Route SMS Sent'
     Location: Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API → anon public

  3. Replace FROM_NUMBER_PLACEHOLDER in 'Send Customer SMS' node:
     Twilio A2P 10DLC campaign not yet approved.
     Replace with the approved Twilio number in E.164 format (e.g. +13212489804)
     when the campaign is approved.

  4. Configure Twilio credentials in n8n:
     n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → Twilio
     Enter: Account SID + Auth Token from Twilio Console
     Update credential id 'twilio-credentials' to match your n8n credential ID.

  5. Activate workflow — do NOT activate before steps 1-4 are complete.

== WORKFLOW BEHAVIOR ==
  - Polls every 30 seconds for jobs where status='En Route' AND en_route_sms_sent=false
  - For each matching job: fetches client business_name, sends customer SMS, marks en_route_sms_sent=true
  - If customer_phone is null: skips SMS, marks en_route_sms_sent=true (no error)
  - SMS failure (Twilio): continueOnFail=true — job status is NEVER reverted
  - Empty result (no En Route jobs): workflow completes silently, nothing to process

== RLS REQUIREMENTS ==
  jobs table: anon SELECT policy (read jobs to poll) — confirmed by E02S05
  jobs table: anon UPDATE policy (write en_route_sms_sent) — confirmed by E03S05
  clients table: anon SELECT policy (read business_name, twilio_number) — confirmed by Sprint 1

== FIELD REFERENCES (n8n 2.x — critical) ==
  ALWAYS: $('Node Name').item.json.fieldname
  NEVER:  $json.fieldname for upstream node data
  Code node output: return [{ json: { ... } }]
  Expression mode: toggle orange '=' icon per field

== URL ENCODING ==
  'En Route' in the Supabase filter URL is encoded as 'En%20Route'
  The URL in 'GET Jobs Pending En Route SMS' already includes this encoding.

== E03S07 MIRROR ==
  E03S07 (On Site SMS) is the mirror of this workflow.
  Differences: status filter = 'On Site', sentinel = on_site_sms_sent,
  SMS template = '[Business Name]: Your technician has arrived.'
  Build E03S07 by duplicating this workflow and changing those three elements.

== TEST PROCEDURE ==
  1. Manually set a test job in Supabase: status='En Route', en_route_sms_sent=false
  2. Ensure customer_phone is set to a valid test number (E.164 format)
  3. Wait up to 30 seconds for poll cycle to fire
  4. Verify: SMS received on customer_phone with correct business name
  5. Verify: en_route_sms_sent=true in Supabase jobs table
  6. Verify: second poll cycle does NOT resend (en_route_sms_sent=true filters it out)
  7. Test null phone: set customer_phone=null on a test job, verify no error and en_route_sms_sent=true after cycle
